title: Render one collapsible panel per background agent result in chat UI
summary: Update chat rendering so each merged/late background agent result gets its own panel (instead of a single combined block), and make those panels collapsed by default with click-to-expand behavior matching the Process pill interaction pattern.
details:
## Why this change
When multiple background agents are spawned in one turn, the chat UI currently shows a single large "Background agent response" block. This hides per-agent boundaries and creates noisy always-open output. Raul requested parity with subagent visibility semantics: 2 agents => 2 response panels, and panels hidden by default.

## Exact source edits
1) **`web-ui/src/pages/ChatPage.js` — replace single-split background parser with multi-panel parser**
- Current behavior (around `renderAssistantContent`, ~470-505):
  - Detects first `Background agent response:` marker.
  - Splits once into `head` + `bgBody`.
  - Renders one always-open `.msg-stage` panel for all background output.
- Edit:
  - Add a parser that extracts **all** background sections in final assistant text.
  - Support both merged-turn payload and late WS injections.
  - Expected marker format after backend update (below):
    - `Background agent response (bg_xxx):` followed by that agent text.
  - Fallback support for legacy marker `Background agent response:` (no id).
  - Render each section as its own collapsible panel with unique DOM id.

2) **`web-ui/src/pages/ChatPage.js` — add collapsible renderer for background panels**
- Add helper functions near render helpers:
  - `parseBackgroundAgentSections(text)`
  - `renderBackgroundAgentPanels(sections)`
  - `toggleBgPanel(id)`
- UI behavior:
  - Default state: collapsed (`display:none` body).
  - Header button per panel: e.g. `Background agent bg_abc123` (or numbered fallback).
  - Click toggles panel body (same style behavior class pattern as existing toggles like `togglePL`).

3) **`web-ui/src/pages/ChatPage.js` — adjust late completion injection (`wsEventBus.on('bg_agent_done')`, ~2939-2968)**
- Current behavior:
  - If any `background agent response:` already exists, it **returns early** and skips injection.
  - This is why second completion doesn’t appear as separate panel.
- Edit:
  - Remove blanket skip.
  - Deduplicate only by **specific bgId marker**.
  - Append using explicit marker per result:
    - `Background agent response (bgId):\n<result>`
  - Continue to inject multiple unique completions into same assistant message.

4) **`src/gateway/routes/chat.router.ts` — include per-agent ids in merged final text (optional but recommended for deterministic grouping)**
- Current behavior in final merge section (~2001-2021):
  - Writes one header `Background agent response:` then appends all merged texts.
- Edit:
  - Emit one labeled section per merged result:
    - `Background agent response (<id>):`
    - `<text>`
  - Keep pending/failed status lines as separate status lines.
- This creates stable parsing in UI and stable dedupe key for late arrivals.

5) **`web-ui/src/styles/components.css` (or `pages.css`) — add styles for collapsible bg panels**
- Add classes:
  - `.bg-panel`, `.bg-panel-toggle`, `.bg-panel-body`, `.bg-panel.open ...`
- Keep visual language consistent with existing `.msg-stage` and process pill controls.

## Deterministic behavior after patch
- If 2 background agents are merged in final reply:
  - Assistant message renders 2 independent background panels.
- If one merged in final reply and another arrives later via `bg_agent_done`:
  - Second panel is appended (not dropped), deduped by `bgId`.
- Panels are collapsed by default and only expand when clicked.
- Existing non-background staged sections (`Initial chat`, `Execution result`, `Final chat`) remain unchanged.
- Legacy replies containing unlabeled `Background agent response:` still render as a single fallback panel.

## Acceptance tests
1) **Two-agent merge in same turn**
- Spawn 2 independent background agents.
- Wait for normal turn completion where join gate merges both.
- Expected: assistant message shows 2 collapsed background panels.

2) **Late completion append**
- Spawn 2 agents; force one to complete after turn done.
- Expected: first panel appears on turn completion; second panel appears later from WS event.
- Expected: no overwrite; no suppression of second panel.

3) **Dedup guard**
- Replay same `bg_agent_done` event with same `bgId`.
- Expected: no duplicate panel for that id.

4) **Collapse UX**
- On render, all background panels closed.
- Click header opens/closes body reliably.

5) **Regression check**
- Process pill toggle still works.
- Regular assistant messages without background sections render unchanged.

## Risks and compatibility
- Risk: parser over-matching if user text manually contains marker phrase.
  - Mitigation: stricter section regex anchored to line starts + optional `(bg_id)` token.
- Risk: backend label format drift.
  - Mitigation: keep fallback parser for legacy unlabeled format.
- Compatibility: additive UI behavior; no API contract break required. Backend id-labeling is recommended for deterministic grouping but UI remains backward-compatible without it.

affectedFiles.description: Parse/render multiple background agent sections, add collapsible panels, and fix bg_agent_done dedupe/injection behavior
Add collapsible background panel styles matching existing chat visual language
Emit per-background-agent labeled sections in merged final text for deterministic UI grouping

estimatedImpact: Makes background agent output understandable at a glance, prevents dropped late results, and reduces chat clutter via default-collapsed panels.
executorPrompt:
Implement proposal exactly across the listed files.

Required edits:
1) In src/gateway/routes/chat.router.ts, update the background merge formatter to emit one labeled section per merged result in the format:
   Background agent response (<id>):
   <result text>
   Keep pending/failed lines as currently implemented.

2) In web-ui/src/pages/ChatPage.js:
   - Replace the single background block split logic in renderAssistantContent with multi-section parsing that supports:
     a) labeled sections: Background agent response (bg_xxx):
     b) legacy unlabeled sections: Background agent response:
   - Render each section as its own collapsible panel (collapsed by default).
   - Add a toggle function for these panels and expose it on window.
   - Update wsEventBus.on('bg_agent_done') handler:
     * Remove global "already has background section" early return.
     * Deduplicate by bgId marker only.
     * Append in labeled format: Background agent response (<bgId>):\n<result>

3) In web-ui/src/styles/components.css, add css classes for the new collapsible background panels, aligned with current msg-stage styling.

Validation steps:
- npm run build
- Verify no TypeScript/build errors.
- Provide concise verification notes covering: multi-panel rendering, default-collapsed behavior, late-injection dedupe by bgId.

Constraints:
- Do not change unrelated chat rendering behavior.
- Keep backward compatibility for existing unlabeled background text.
- Keep changes minimal and deterministic.
